exemplar.title: How does Sport England's "This Girl Can" campaign challenge women's fear of judgment and encourage physical activity through their representation of female bodies?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Sport England's "This Girl Can" campaign challenge women's fear of judgment and encourage physical activity through their representation of female bodies?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Solid contextual knowledge of the 2015 Sport England campaign linked to body-positivity and feminist theory
Sustained interpretation of visual and textual features against the line of inquiry
Relevant and generally effective textual references and citations
Integration of Luna Dolezalâs concept of body shame enriches theoretical foundation
weaknesses: Analysis not exhaustive or fully persuasive for the highest bandâwider marketing context and counter-readings are only hinted at
Limited range of examples; deeper explanation of some conceptual points (e.g. athleticism as femininity) is needed
Occasional lack of specificity about visual details (layout, color, camera angle)
Inconsistent page and figure citations reduce academic rigor
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates good knowledge and understanding with sustained interpretation of implications (Band 4). Contextual framing is strong and textual support is generally effective, but deeper, more persuasive analysis and broader contextual scope are absent, preventing a top-band score.

strengths: Insightful linking of slogans to feminist philosophy (e.g. Descartes inversion, mind/body dualism)
Consistent theoretical framing using Youngâs phenomenology to evaluate authorial choices
Philosophical reframing of campaign rhetoric offers a convincing, critical perspective
Sustained evaluation of how imagery de-sexualises and normalises âjiggleâ
weaknesses: Omission of certain visual specifics (lighting, color palettes, typography) limits the depth of compositional analysis
Discussion could further address target-audience demographics and marketing strategy
Occasional reliance on description where deeper evaluation of authorâs strategic choices is possible
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis moves beyond description to provide consistently insightful and convincing evaluation of textual features and authorial choices, particularly through its philosophical and phenomenological framing. Minor lapses in visual detail do not undermine its overall persuasiveness, meriting a top-band score (Band 5).

strengths: Clear introduction-body-conclusion structure anchored by a focused line of inquiry
Logical progression through individual poster analyses with effective transitions
Smooth integration of examples and citations within sentences
Cohesive argument that continually returns to the central research question
weaknesses: Lacks a comparative synthesis section to draw thematic links across posters
Conclusion reiterates findings rather than extending implications or proposing further inquiry
Some digressions (e.g. extended definition of body-positivity) slow narrative momentum
A few long paragraphs could be broken up for enhanced readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay is well organized and mostly cohesive, with an adequately developed line of inquiry and effective integration of support (Band 4). Deeper synthesis and a more expansive conclusion would be required for the highest band.

strengths: Fluent, academic prose with varied sentence structures and appropriate vocabulary
Meaning is consistently clear; register is formal and suits the HL analytical task
Discipline-specific terminology and proper citation style reinforce academic tone
weaknesses: Noticeable typographical and spelling errors (e.g. âheticismâ, âfeminimityâ)
Comma splices, missing articles, and minor grammar lapses occur
Inconsistent MLA formatting and figure citation conventions distract from precision
Occasional informal phrasing slightly weakens an otherwise formal register
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Language is clear and generally accurate, with a formal register appropriate to the task despite sporadic errors in grammar, spelling, and formatting. This performance aligns with Band 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Strengths:** Poster slogans, theorist quotations, and paraphrased research are smoothly embedded within sentences (âYoung observesâ¦â, âThe bold text âSweating like a pigâ¦â uses similesâ¦â).  In-text references avoid stand-alone quotation drops, and MLA-style citations are visible.

**Limitations:** Minor lapses occur: âunder these gloves is a beautiful manicureâ is mis-quoted (âareâ in appendix), and some page references for images are missing.  Nevertheless, examples generally sit inside analytic sentences rather than being appended.

Integration is therefore âmostly well integrated,â placing the work firmly in Band 4.

**Strengths:** The student demonstrates solid contextual knowledge of the 2015 Sport England campaign and links it to key concepts in body-positivity and feminist theory (Young, Leboeuf, Dolezal).  Each poster is accurately described and the social intention of the slogans is interpreted, showing how they combat âfear of judgement.â  The discussion repeatedly returns to the stated line of inquiry, so understanding is sustained rather than occasional.

**Limitations:** While competent, the discussion is not exhaustive or really âpersuasiveâ enough for the top band: wider marketing context, audience segmentation, or counter-readings are only hinted at.  Some conceptual points (e.g. âreframing athleticism as an integral part of femininityâ) would benefit from deeper explanation.

Taken together, the work aligns best with Band 4: good knowledge and understanding, with sustained (but not compelling) interpretation.

**Strengths:** The essay follows a clear introduction-body-conclusion structure anchored by a focused line of inquiry.  Each body section deals with one figure, creating logical progression.  Transitional phrases (âIn Figure 2â¦â, âThis rejection of perfectionism culminatesâ¦â) maintain cohesion, and the argument is brought back to the central question in the conclusion.

**Limitations:** While coherent, the development could be deeper: a comparative paragraph synthesising findings across posters is absent, and the conclusion briefly reiterates earlier points rather than extending them.  A few digressions (definition of body-positivity) slow momentum.

Given these factors, organization is mostly cohesive with an adequately developed line of inquiry, aligning with Band 4.

**Strengths:** Each analytical point is supported by a concrete poster reference or a cited theoretical source.  Citations are accurate, follow a consistent style and appear in the Works Cited; slogans are quoted verbatim; theorists are clearly attributed.  The examples chosen (boxing image, âSweating like a pigâ¦â, âI jiggleâ¦â) are well matched to the claims being made, so support is effective rather than perfunctory.

**Limitations:** Evidence is restricted to three posters and a small cluster of academic texts; no details of layout, colour palette or camera angle are quoted directly.  The occasional descriptive phrase (âher body is active, strong, and purposefulâ) is not always tied to a specific visual detail.

Overall, references are relevant and generally strengthen the argument, placing the work in Band 4.

**Strengths:** The student maintains a formal, analytical register appropriate for an HL essay, avoiding colloquialisms and directly addressing the scholarly debate.  Citations and discipline-specific terminology support an academic style.

**Limitations:** Occasional informal phrasings (âsends a clear messageâ) and minor consistency issues with MLA formatting slightly weaken stylistic control, but do not undermine appropriateness.

Overall, register is consistently appropriate, satisfying Band 4 requirements.

**Strengths:** The prose is fluent and academic, with varied sentence structures and appropriate vocabulary (âhyper-glamorous,â âreframes athleticismâ).  Meaning is consistently clear.

**Limitations:** Noticeable typographical and spelling slips appear (âat heticism,â âfeminimity,â âjuxtaposesâ).  Comma splices and missing articles occur, but they seldom impede comprehension.  With these lapses, the language cannot be called âvery clear and preciseâ throughout.

Consequently, clarity and accuracy meet the standard for Band 4: clear language with good accuracy despite sporadic errors.

**Strengths:** The essay moves beyond description by linking slogans to feminist philosophy (Descartes inversion, reclaiming âsweatâ) and by commenting on how images de-sexualise or normalise âjiggle.â  There are moments of insight, e.g. identifying the campaignâs disruption of the mind/body hierarchy.

**Limitations:** Analytical depth is uneven; camera framing, typeface, colour, and composition are acknowledged only implicitly, so some paragraphs slip back into description.  Evaluation of the authorâs *choices* (why Sport England selects certain imagery for target audiences) is present but not sustained.

Overall the analysis is appropriate and periodically insightful, matching Band 4 rather than the consistently convincing Band 5.

annotations.comment: The title is clear and specific, effectively framing the focus on Sport Englandâs campaign and textual analysis, establishing an organized research scope.
The line of inquiry is well defined, posing a clear research question that maintains focus on judgment and representation throughout the essay.
The introduction provides comprehensive context on body positivity and previews how visual and textual features interplay in the campaign.
The definition of body positivity, while informative, could be more concise to strengthen the introductionâs analytical focus rather than broad description.
The analysis links Youngâs phenomenology with the posterâs depiction of female strength, moving beyond surface description to evaluate authorial choices.
The discussion omits visual specifics such as lighting, colour palette, or framing, limiting the depth of analysis of the posterâs composition.
Typographical errors (âaticismâ, âfeminimityâ) distract from clarity; proofreading is needed to maintain precision in academic prose.
Further explanation of how reframing athleticism advances feminist theory would strengthen the interpretation of that conceptual point.
Integration of Luna Dolezalâs concept of body shame effectively supports the argument and enriches the theoretical foundation.
The poster reference (Figure 2) lacks specific page or appendix citation, which would improve academic rigor and traceability.
Consider evaluating Sport Englandâs choice of informal similes (âpigâ, âfoxâ) in light of target audience demographics to deepen analysis of authorial intent.
Compound noun âself-perceptionâ requires a hyphen; minor grammar lapses affect the formal tone.
Effectively connects back to Youngâs spatial theory, demonstrating sustained analytical evaluation of the campaignâs rhetoric.
The allusion to Descartes is insightful, reframing the campaignâs slogan philosophically to challenge mind/body dualism convincingly.
Analysis overlooks design elements like colour contrast or typography, which could reveal additional layers in audience engagement.
The conclusion reiterates prior points but stops short of synthesizing implications or proposing avenues for further inquiry.
Include a comparative synthesis paragraph highlighting cross-poster thematic links to strengthen overall organizational cohesion.
Discussing the campaignâs wider marketing context or counter-readings would add depth to the evaluation of its societal impact.
Ensure the âWorks Citedâ entries are consistently alphabetized by author surname to adhere to MLA standards.
Add the publisherâs location for Descartesâ text to complete the bibliographic entry per MLA format.
Provide an access date for the JSTOR source to comply with citation conventions for online journal articles.
Specify the appendix figure numbers for images in the âImagesâ list to improve clarity and traceability.
Formatting âFigure 1 (Appendix 1)â consistentlyâcapitalizing âFigureââwill uphold formal academic style.
Break up the lengthy paragraph into focused sections to enhance readability and topic coherence.
Consider adding explicit transitional phrases to reinforce the progression between poster analyses.
Verify each slogan quote against the original campaign materials for verbatim accuracy to uphold analytic precision.
Ensure consistency in page citations (e.g., Leboeuf p. 113 vs p. 115) to avoid confusion in textual support.
Check punctuation around parenthetical phrases to prevent sentence fragments and maintain clarity.
